scenario: Phishing → service persistence → process injection → credential theft → lateral movement

findings.title: Spear-phishing macro execution from Q4_Invoice.docm
findings.description: User j.morgan opened a malicious Office document containing a macro that spawned powershell.exe.

findings.title: Malicious service EvtSysSvc registered for persistence
findings.description: A service named EvtSysSvc was registered pointing to C:\Windows\Temp\svchost_helper.exe (T1543.003).

findings.title: Process injection into svchost.exe from powershell.exe
findings.description: malfind detected an RWX VAD region in svchost.exe (PID 1488) with shellcode signature consistent with Cobalt Strike beacon. PSScan found a process powershell.exe (PID 4096) not present in PSList — DKOM-hidden parent.

findings.title: Credential theft via mimikatz_x64.exe
findings.description: AmCache and Prefetch both record execution of mimikatz_x64.exe from C:\Windows\Temp\.

findings.title: Lateral movement attempt via RDP to 10.10.20.5
findings.description: Event ID 4624 type 10 from j.morgan to 10.10.20.5; netscan shows TCP/3389 connection from PID 4096.
